mechanism_narrative: Positive monthly momentum in retail sales signals near-term revenue growth for listed firms; when RSAFS is rising on a 3-month basis, S&P 500 earnings expectations are revised upward and 20-day forward returns are positive.
failure_condition: Reject if, in walk-forward tests, 3-month RSAFS growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window or if positive RSAFS momentum is followed by positive returns in no more than the unconditional base rate.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS increases equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions before earnings adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 75 bp over 6 months and crosses above 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deterioration in household income and recession risk; when UNRATE increases sharply, equity investors lower expected cash flows and S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if the trigger has nonnegative average excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts combined with rising short rates identifies rate-sensitive demand destruction; when HOUST declines while FEDFUNDS rises, construction-linked activity and household wealth weaken, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months while FEDFUNDS rises by more than 50 bp over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value for 2005-01-01 was 2159.0 and FEDFUNDS for 2005-02-01 was 2.5
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-down and FEDFUNDS-up trigger does not underperform the standalone HOUST-down signal by at least 25 bp on average over 60 trading days or if triggered returns are positive in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improving operating leverage and cyclical earnings breadth; positive INDPRO momentum leads investors to mark up near-term cash-flow expectations, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO momentum has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns in two consecutive 3-year windows or if average returns during positive momentum do not exceed baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration without an offsetting rise in policy rates erodes real household income and raises the probability of later aggressive tightening; when CPIAUCSL growth rises faster than FEDFUNDS, 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises above its prior 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS increases by less than 25 bp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value for 2005-01-01 was 191.3 and FEDFUNDS for 2005-02-01 was 2.5
regime_hint: inflation pressure with policy behind the curve
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-acceleration and FEDFUNDS-lag interaction has nonnegative 60-day average excess returns or if its interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth together with a falling unemployment rate identifies broad labor-income expansion; when PAYEMS momentum is positive and UNRATE declines, equity cash-flow expectations improve and 5-day S&P 500 returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive while UNRATE falls by at least 0.1 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value for 2005-02-01 was 132843.0 and UNRATE was 5.4
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-down trigger fails to produce a higher 5-day win rate than the unconditional sample by at least 3 percentage points or has negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend real GDP growth confirms broad final-demand strength and supports aggregate earnings; when GDPC1 growth crosses above a moderate expansion threshold, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available vintage quarter was 2004-10-01 with value 10993.3
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth above 2.5% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in no more than the unconditional base rate or if the signal has Rank IC below 0.02 over two consecutive 4-year windows.
